reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome — native Urdu (Perso-Arabic) tokenises to non-empty input_ids via a tokenizer algorithm proven byte-identical to the real VitsTokenizer, and the real VitsModel returns non-silent audio; RTL rendering (lang=ur dir=rtl) applied to all Urdu content; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow; page load conformance tested pass), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile, dedicated module Worker). BEFORE building, the checkpoint's tokenizer_config.json was confirmed is_uroman:false (language:"urd-script_arabic", add_blank:true) with a 58-symbol native Perso-Arabic vocab (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor). The real VitsModel (naklitechie/mms-tts-ur-ONNX, fp32, loaded via AutoModel from model.onnx at the repo root) run on native Urdu produced 127 input tokens and a real Float32Array waveform — 90368 samples @ 16000 Hz (5.65 s), peak 0.7517, rms 0.1875, 100% non-zero (non-silent), in 4955 ms; no ONNX abort (unlike the defective mms-tts-kan export). NOT faked, NOT silent, NOT empty.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a mono waveform Float32Array at 16 kHz. The VitsModel returned {waveform, spectrogram}; the page reads waveform.data, encodes it to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio>, and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Urdu speech (peak 0.7517, 100% non-zero).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language + DISTINCT-SCRIPT VITS demo: the Urdu checkpoint (own native Perso-Arabic char vocab of 58 symbols + Urdu-trained weights, canonical tag urd-script_arabic). Urdu is written right-to-left in the Nastaliq script — the first RTL MMS-TTS demo in the catalogue. Note the vocab quirk: the pad/blank token content is the letter 'د', which the canonical vocab maps to id 0, so a literal 'د' and the interleaved blank share id 0 — faithfully reproduced from facebook/mms-tts-urd-script_arabic.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (naklitechie/mms-tts-ur-ONNX, linked), that it is an ONNX export of canonical facebook/mms-tts-urd-script_arabic (also linked), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (fp32, model.onnx), size (~114 MB), license (CC-BY-NC 4.0) AND the is_uroman:false native Perso-Arabic RTL fact. Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: PROVENANCE + TOKENISER honesty: the 'How the API works' section discloses that this export ships model.onnx at the repo root and omits tokenizer.json, so the worker loads the real VitsModel and reproduces the deterministic (is_uroman:false) VitsTokenizer — using the same algorithm VERIFIED byte-for-byte identical to transformers.js's real tokenizer on Xenova/mms-tts-hin (realLen 45==manualLen 45, identical id sequence). The Urdu vocab.json is byte-identical to canonical facebook/mms-tts-urd-script_arabic. CC-BY-NC 4.0 noted in at-a-glance AND footer.

rubric.evidence: The page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (mount + model {modelId:'naklitechie/mms-tts-ur-ONNX', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:114}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on naklitechie/mms-tts-ur-ONNX so the ~114 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), and the worker surfaces an on-page error if no Urdu characters are recognised in the input.
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the RTL Urdu textarea (lang=ur dir=rtl), Urdu sample chips (تعارف/سلام/موسم/خبر/اعلان/مقولہ, each dir=rtl), the بولیں button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→ur) + the TTS with an RTL translation output.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built Hindi/Tamil/Marathi mms-tts demos, extended with correct RTL affordances for the Urdu inputs and outputs.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js VitsModel; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The tokenisation uses the algorithm proven identical to the canonical VitsTokenizer, so the input the model sees is exactly what the real pipeline would feed it — no fabricated or mis-tokenised input, and no Latin-input-as-native fallback.

All buttons have accessible names (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Urdu speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); ALL Urdu content carries lang=ur dir=rtl so the browser shapes the Nastaliq script and orders it right-to-left correctly, and screen readers announce it as Urdu. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: RTL correctness is the headline accessibility concern for this demo: the textarea, sample chips and the multi-model translation output all set dir=rtl; the layout uses logical properties so it mirrors cleanly without a separate stylesheet.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) AND desktop (1280×800), both light+dark. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps. The RTL text fields use logical properties (text-align:start) so they stay within bounds in both directions.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Mobile download cost (~114 MB fp32) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in web worker; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis (tokenisation + VitsModel inference) runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); UI responsive during synthesis; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: right-to-left RTL text rendering dir attribute logical properties for Arabic/Nastaliq script
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use logical properties (inline-size, margin-inline, text-align:start) so layouts mirror correctly for RTL; set dir=rtl on the RTL content itself rather than reversing flex order.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — every Urdu field/output carries dir=rtl and lang=ur; the shared CSS uses logical properties throughout (inline-size, block-size, margin-inline-end) and text-align:start on RTL fields, so the grid/flex layout mirrors cleanly with no fixed physical widths and no horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: dir=rtl on textarea/chips/translation; 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check (desktop clean · mobile clean)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling; Urdu Perso-Arabic script rendering
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; mark language + direction for correct script shaping and announcement.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; all Urdu content carries lang=ur dir=rtl for correct Nastaliq shaping and RTL order.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; lang=ur dir=rtl on Urdu content

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label describing it as generated Urdu speech; the numeric readout (input tokens/samples/rate/duration) also conveys the same information as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readout duplicate the visual

openQuestions: This checkpoint ships only an fp32 ONNX (~114 MB) — a quantised (q8) Urdu export would cut the mobile download ~3x; worth watching for an onnx-community/Xenova Urdu export with the full tokenizer + quantised variants.
Does M2M100 en→ur produce natural, well-formed Urdu for the multi-model page across a wider set of English inputs, and does it emit clean Perso-Arabic (not Latin/Hindi-script) output for all of them?

followUpGoals.goal: If a fully-packaged Urdu MMS-TTS export lands (onnx/ layout + tokenizer.json + q8), migrate the worker to the plain pipeline() path and add a q8 dtype option to shrink the mobile download.
Add more RTL-script MMS-TTS demos (e.g. Persian/Farsi, Sindhi) using the same verified manual-tokenizer + dir=rtl path, once each is confirmed to produce non-silent audio.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-urdu (naklitechie/mms-tts-ur-ONNX, text-to-speech, fp32, ~114 MB — the URDU VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language + first RIGHT-TO-LEFT demo). CRITICAL pre-check: is_uroman:false with a 58-symbol NATIVE PERSO-ARABIC vocab byte-identical to canonical facebook/mms-tts-urd-script_arabic. RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: native Urdu tokenised to 127 NON-EMPTY input_ids and the real VitsModel returned non-silent Urdu audio (90368 samples @16 kHz, 5.65 s, peak 0.7517, 100% non-zero, {waveform, spectrogram}); no ONNX abort. Because this export omits tokenizer.json, the worker reproduces the deterministic (is_uroman:false) VitsTokenizer using the same algorithm VERIFIED byte-for-byte identical to the real tokenizer on a control language (Hindi: 45==45, identical ids). RTL: all Urdu content carries lang=ur dir=rtl with logical-property layout. responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Ladder: basics (type Urdu, hear it), practical (Urdu narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→ur translate, then MMS speaks the Urdu). MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
